canonical_definition: The measurement instrument MAGPIE is characterized in the provided literature as a corpus of potentially idiomatic expressions, with each expression labeled as either figurative or literal. The data points to its use in evaluating how models handle non-literal language. Specifically, one paper describes using MAGPIE to test whether global affinity can distinguish between figurative and literal usage. Another paper reports adapting the dataset to measure the task of Multi-word expression identification.
papers: Grounded Semantic Role Labelling from Synthetic Multimodal Data for Situated Robot Commands
Language Models as Causal Effect Generators
SAE-SSV: Supervised Steering in Sparse Representation Spaces for Reliable Control of Language Models

definitions.text: A corpus of potentially idiomatic expressions labeled as figurative or literal, used to test whether global affinity distinguishes figurative from literal usage.
definitions.paper: Language Models as Causal Effect Generators

relationships.paper: Grounded Semantic Role Labelling from Synthetic Multimodal Data for Situated Robot Commands
relationships.evidence: Since our goal is identification, we adapt the dataset for this task, as detailed in Appendix A, where we also describe some filtering we apply. (Section 3.2)

emnlp25: Grounded Semantic Role Labelling from Synthetic Multimodal Data for Situated Robot Commands
